title: On Clemency
author: Seneca
description: Seneca's "Of Clemency" is a philosophical treatise that delves into the nature of mercy and the moral imperatives underpinning governance. Written in the context of the Roman Empire, the work reflects Stoic principles, advocating for a ruler's obligation to exhibit clemency as a demonstration of true strength and virtuous leadership. The text serves as a prescriptive guide for emperors, particularly directed at Nero, advising them on the delicate balance between power and morality, and how justi...
